summary: DeVault argues against building new federation protocols like ActivityPub for git collaboration, contending that email already provides everything needed for decentralized, federated development workflows. He points to the vast ecosystem of projects—Linux, gcc, PostgreSQL, ffmpeg—that already use email-based collaboration successfully. He advocates for improving existing email-based tools and building web forges like sr.ht that smooth over email's rough edges rather than creating a second, incompatible federation system. The post positions email as superior to ActivityPub in maturity, ease of implementation, user empowerment, and resilience.
key_points: Git's email-based workflow is already federated and decentralized, making ActivityPub-based alternatives redundant
ActivityPub would create a second, incompatible federation while ignoring the existing productive federation used by foundational open source projects
Email is easier to implement than ActivityPub, with mature tooling available in nearly every language's standard library
Email puts power in users' hands—data lives in their inbox, not on a server that could disappear
Web forges should embrace email as their communication protocol rather than replace it, smoothing over usability issues for newcomers
money_quotes: But get this: git is already federated and decentralized!
ActivityPub would build a second, incompatible federation of projects, while ignoring the already productive federation which powers many of our most important open source projects.
These projects are incredibly important, foundational projects upon which our open source empire is built, and the tools they use already provide an open, federated protocol for us to talk to.
Not only is email better, but it's also easier to implement.
I bet every single person who reads this article already has an email address, so the setup time approaches zero.
Email was designed when networks were measured in bits per second and often connected through a single unreliable route!
I'm suggesting that we should embrace it and solve these problems instead of throwing out the baby with the bathwater.
key_insight: Rather than building new federation protocols for git collaboration, the open source community should improve the email-based workflow that already powers its most important projects.
